refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on energy consumption per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and calculate energy_per_ton to verify it meets the target E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency within target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy metering sensors and telemetry for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to optimize energy use.
DP3: Develop feedback control loops with operator and teleoperation interfaces.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 + DP2 + DP3 combined to address monitoring, optimization, and control for energy efficiency.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency between DP1 and DP3
Control algorithm dependency on accurate energy data from DP1
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via well-defined telemetry and control interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and reliable energy sensors are available.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting real-time performance.
Assumes operator and teleoperation systems can respond to feedback.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure modular design of control algorithms to isolate complexity and allow incremental verification.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components including energy sensors and vehicle control systems responsible for energy efficiency.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends energy consumption and operational data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands from Teleoperation module to adjust vehicle behavior for energy optimization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as energy sensors and control algorithms are physically and logically within Vehicle.
Contains system-level monitoring and KPI aggregation but less direct control.
Receives data and sends commands but does not contain core energy control logic.
Minimal involvement in energy efficiency; focused on safety alerts.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency control is best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and enable direct hardware/software integration.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module may increase integration risk.
Dependency on Teleoperation commands requires robust interface management.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider splitting control algorithm into submodules if complexity grows; ensure clear interface contracts with Teleoperation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FRs are well-defined and mapped to candidate DPs that logically satisfy the energy efficiency requirement.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface diffusion between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Control command diffusion between Teleoperation and Vehicle.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment of core DPs with manageable diffusion through known interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy consumption and tonnage at Vehicle level and validating telemetry data at NGHE Solution.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy monitoring and control to Vehicle; system-level KPI aggregation in NGHE Solution.

recommended_revisions: Define detailed interface specifications for Telemetry and Tasking to reduce diffusion risks.
Plan for modularization within Vehicle if control complexity increases.

decision.justification: Functional decomposition and DP selection are sound, but IM assignment requires refinement to address interface diffusion and complexity management.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency within target.

revise.why: To better manage interface diffusion and complexity within Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

assumptions: Operator and teleoperation systems can respond to control feedback.
open_risks: Complexity of control algorithms within Vehicle.
Interface robustness between Vehicle and Teleoperation.
evidence_needed: Interface protocol specifications and test results.
